
::: {.paper-table}
**Table 31. C.4 Candidate-gene studies — a single polymorphism determines your fate**

| Claim (at published magnitude) | Implication if true | Source |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 5-HTTLPR short allele + life stress → depression | Whether you develop depression after a bad year is substantially determined by which variant of a single gene you carry | Caspi et al. [@caspi2003] |
| MAOA low-activity + maltreatment → antisocial behavior | A single polymorphism substantially moderates whether childhood abuse produces a violent adult (“warrior gene”) | Caspi et al. [@caspi2002] |
| DRD4 7-repeat → novelty seeking, ADHD, parenting | A single dopamine-receptor variant substantially predicts personality and clinical outcomes across domains | Benjamin et al. [@benjamin1996] and many derivatives |
| COMT val/met → cognition, pain, psychosis | A single amino-acid substitution substantially predicts cognitive performance, pain sensitivity, and psychosis risk | Extensive 2000s literature |
| OXTR polymorphisms → empathy, trust, parenting | Variants in the oxytocin-receptor gene substantially predict social-cognitive outcomes | Numerous 2005–2015 |
:::
